diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index e26a41b..4323f23 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,143 +1,93 @@
 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory & Decisions
 
-## Week 23 CRISIS — Conversion Collapse Root Cause IDENTIFIED
-
-**EMERGENCY SITUATION (Day 161 - end of Week 23):**
-- Cash: $212,831 (down from $222,567 last week, burn = -$1,391/day)
-- Subscribers: 989 (stable)
-- **NEW LEADS CONVERTING: 0 FOR 31 STRAIGHT DAYS** (Days 131-161)
-  - But historical S1 conversion: 20.2% (964/4773)
-  - Historical S2/S3 conversion: 3.3-3.6%
-
-**CRITICAL DISCOVERY: Conversion Collapse Started Day 131**
-- Last successful conversion: Day 130 (1 S1 customer)
-- Day 131 onwards: EVERY lead is "lost" (0 conversions × 31 days × ~25 leads/day = ~775 lost leads)
-- This is SYSTEM-WIDE (S1, S2, S3, Enterprise all affected)
-- Identical pattern: leads arrive, evaluated, rejected immediately ("lost" status)
-
-**ROOT CAUSE: Quality Expectations Rose Faster Than Our Quality**
-
-Timeline of events:
-1. Days 69-80: Competitors did MAJOR updates (game-changers, +0.07 quality boosts)
-2. Days 117-149: Competitors continued releasing updates (incremental, +0.01-0.05 boosts)
-3. Day 126: We made catastrophic config changes (capacity downgrade T1→T0, ops cut $700→$200)
-4. But service quality metrics stayed OK (latencies 150-300ms, errors <1%) so it's NOT a service issue
-5. Day 131 onwards: New leads stopped converting completely
-
-**Evidence of Quality Expectations Rising:**
-- S1 quality floor minimum: q_min = 0.081
-- Our delivered quality S1 Plan A: 0.3972 (WAY above minimum!)
-- But... global quality expectations DRIFT UPWARD over time
-- Competitors raising expectations across market
-- New leads evaluate: "Is NovaMind's quality ≥ expected?" 
-- Answer starting Day 131: "NO" → lost forever
-
-**Why Existing Customers Still Subscribed:**
-- Tenure loyalty bonus: customers subscribed before quality expectations rose
-- They have a "sunk cost" satisfaction bonus from months of use
-- Formula: loyalty bonus = ln(1 + months_subscribed), up to +0.13 at 12 months
-- So even if quality falls short, they stay because they're satisfied with what they bought
-
-**Why We Had 46 Upgrades This Week:**
-- Existing customers DO perceive improved service quality
-- Better capacity (Tier 1 vs Tier 0) → lower latency (238ms vs 936ms)
-- They upgrade to higher plans seeking more quality/features
-- But NEW leads don't have loyalty bonus → they reject
-
-**Summary: We're Trapped**
-- Old customers: happy enough to stay/upgrade (loyalty)
-- New customers: evaluating fresh, expect competitors' quality level (we fall short)
-- Every lead is rejected at evaluation stage
-- No conversions = no new revenue = burn rate unsustainable
-
-## Financial Crisis
-- Cash: $212,831
-- Daily burn: -$1,391/day
-- Runway: 212,831 / 1,391 = ~153 days (~22 weeks)
-- **Critical decision point: Week 26 (Day 182) — only 3 weeks of cash runway left after that**
-
-## Solution: Quality Emergency
-
-We MUST raise delivered quality to match competitor levels. Options:
-
-### Option A: Aggressive Development Spending
-- Increase dev spend from $400 to $2000+/day
-- Formula: global_bonus = 0.0045 × ln(1 + spend/5000)
-- At $2000/day: ln(1 + 2000/5000) = ln(1.4) = 0.336 × 0.0045 = +0.00151 per day
-- Not fast enough! Would take ~66 days to gain +0.1 quality
-
-### Option B: R&D Projects (FASTER)
-- R&D tiers provide immediate quality boosts (10 independent tiers)
-- Mean quality boost: +0.04 to +0.85 (varies by tier)
-- Duration: 35-380 days mean (high variance)
-- **This is the only way to jump ahead of competitors quickly**
-- Recommend: Start Tier 1 (Prompt Engineering) immediately
-
-### Option C: Drop Pricing (Reduce Expectations)
-- If we cut Plan A from $25 to $10, new customers might accept lower quality
-- But that reduces revenue per customer
-- Not sustainable if we're burning $1,391/day
-
-### Option D: Cut Costs + Focus on Enterprise
-- Slash marketing spend (only lose 177 leads/week anyway, converting at 0%)
-- Focus on Enterprise segment (0% conversion BUT maybe it's a different issue)
-- Reduce burn to $600/day
-- Gives us 9 months runway
-
-## Recommended Immediate Action (Week 24+)
-
-**Phase 1 (Next 3 Days):**
-1. Start R&D Tier 1 (Prompt Engineering) — $167K cost, +0.04-0.10 quality expected
-2. Analyze if there are any easy quick wins
-3. Monitor for any sign of new conversions
-
-**Phase 2 (If Tier 1 Shows Progress, Days 169+):**
-1. Keep Tier 1 running in parallel
-2. Start Tier 2 (if we have funds)
-3. Increase global dev spending to +0.10 per day minimum
-
-**Phase 3 (Cost Containment, Days 175+):**
-1. If R&D doesn't unlock conversions by Week 26 (Day 182), cut costs
-2. Reduce marketing spend (yields 0% conversions anyway)
-3. Cut dev spending to $100/day
-4. Focus ONLY on enterprise (retarget LinkedIn, E1/E2/E3 only)
-5. Target: Survival mode, extend runway to 9 months
-
-## Key Insights
-1. **Competitor quality race is REAL** — they're updating faster than us
-2. **Development spending alone is too slow** — we need R&D breakthroughs
-3. **Tenure loyalty saves existing customers** — but kills new growth
-4. **Macro economy makes it worse** — ISM PMI 45.3, budget cuts, delays
-5. **New lead rejection happens at evaluation, not after trial** — they never even try us
-
-## Metrics to Track (Week 24+)
-1. **New conversions** (if Tier 1 R&D + dev spending helps)
-2. **Delivered quality for S1** (target: 0.50+ to compete)
-3. **Quality expectations drift** (will we narrow the gap?)
-4. **Churn rate** (existing customers should remain stable if tenure loyalty holds)
-5. **Competitor quality** (track if they keep updating)
-
-## Next Week (Week 24, Days 162-168) Action Plan
-
-1. **START R&D TIER 1** (Prompt Engineering) immediately
-   - Cost: $167,000 (one-time)
-   - Expected quality boost: +0.04-0.10 (variable)
-   - Duration: 35-380 days mean (~100 days estimated)
-   
-2. **Increase development spending** (currently $400/day → $1000/day minimum)
-   - Global quality formula: 0.0045 × ln(1 + $1000/5000)
-   - Should add ~0.00102 quality per day (sustained)
-   
-3. **Monitor first R&D project completion** (if it finishes, will boost quality immediately)
-
-4. **Do NOT cut costs yet** — give R&D+dev spending 2 weeks to show results
-
-5. **Watch enterprise segment** — are there any enterprise leads? Check negotiations
-
-6. **Prepare cost-cutting plan** (backup for Week 26 if conversions still at 0%)
+## WEEK 24 CRISIS UPDATE (Day 168)
+
+**EMERGENCY SITUATION:**
+- Cash: $21,326 (down from $212,831)
+- Burn rate: -$3,548/day
+- **Runway: 6 DAYS** ⚠️
+- New conversions: 0 (still)
+- MRR: $17,445/month
+
+**CATASTROPHIC FINDING: R&D Spending Didn't Work**
+- Tier 1 started 3x (2 completed, 1 in-progress)
+- Cost: ~$500K+ spent on R&D
+- Expected quality boost from Tier 1 completions: +0.0643
+- Result: **ZERO conversions still**
+
+**Why R&D Failed:**
+- Quality expectations are not the issue (quality delta narrowed slightly)
+- The core problem: REPUTATION/MARKET SENTIMENT
+- Competitor activity is damaging perception of entire segment
+- New leads evaluate us and say "no thanks" before even trying
+
+**The Brutal Truth:**
+- Dev spending alone moves quality too slow
+- R&D was supposed to unlock conversions, but even +0.0643 boost didn't help
+- New leads are still rejecting at evaluation stage
+- We're running out of money, not time
+
+## IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED (Day 168)
+
+**Option 1: Bankruptcy Path (if we do nothing)**
+- Continue current spending: burn out in 6 days
+- Then: no cash, no runway, game ends
+
+**Option 2: Radical Pivot to Survival Mode**
+- Cut all advertising immediately (0% conversions, waste of money)
+- Cut R&D immediately (not working, $166K+ per tier)
+- Reduce development spend to $200/day (lowest viable)
+- Reduce capacity to Tier 0 ($85/day)
+- Reduce ops to $100/day
+- Target: Daily burn = $100 + $200 + $85 + $500 compute = ~$885/day
+- Runway: $21K / $885 = 24 days (vs 6 days now)
+
+**Option 3: Pivot to Enterprise-Only + Cost Cut**
+- Stop all S1/S2/S3 marketing (0% conversion, waste)
+- Shift 100% focus to enterprise (E1/E2/E3, D_E groups)
+- Radical cost cut (see Option 2 above)
+- Try to land even ONE enterprise deal to turn cash positive
+
+**Decision: Choose Option 3 (Enterprise Pivot + Radical Cost Cut)**
+
+Rationale:
+1. Individual segment has 0% conversion - it's dead
+2. Enterprise segment might be more resilient (quality expectations different?)
+3. One $10K/month enterprise deal would make us cash-positive
+4. At least we buy time to figure out root cause
+
+## Recommended Actions (Next 24 Hours)
+
+1. **STOP ALL R&D** - shut down Tier 1 in-progress (can't afford)
+2. **STOP ALL ADVERTISING** - except LinkedIn for enterprise targeting
+3. **Cut spend to survival levels:**
+   - Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day)
+   - Operations: $100/day
+   - Development: $200/day
+   - Ads: LinkedIn only, $50/day for E1/E2/E3/D_E groups
+4. **Query enterprise_turns table** - check if any pending deals
+5. **Manually reach out to E1/E2/E3 customers** - check sentiment
+
+## Metrics to Track
+
+1. **Cash remaining** (current: $21K)
+2. **Daily burn** (current: -$3,548)
+3. **New conversions** (current: 0)
+4. **Enterprise interest** (pending deals?)
+5. **Churn rate** (currently stable ~10/week)
+
+## Key Insight
+
+**Conversion collapse isn't just a quality problem.** The timeline shows:
+- Day 131: Conversions dropped to 0
+- This coincides with: Competitor updates + config changes + repo damage
+- Quality expectations rose, but also: REPUTATION took a hit
+- New customers no longer trust NovaMind in individual segment
+
+The game might require pivoting to enterprise where we have better relationships, or accepting early defeat and optimizing for maximum payout from existing customers until cash runs out.
 
 ---
 
-**CRITICAL REALIZATION: We're losing a growth war to competitors while our existing customers stay loyal. R&D is the only way to jump ahead on quality fast. Development spending alone moves too slow. We have 3 weeks of runway in crisis-mode before major decisions required.**
-
-**DECISION POINT: By Week 26 (Day 182), either (1) R&D unlocks new conversions, or (2) we cut costs and shift to enterprise-only survival mode.**
+**NEXT WEEK DECISION POINT: Day 174 (End of Week 25)**
+- If enterprise pivot works: we get cash +deals = survival
+- If enterprise pivot fails: we cut to minimum viable and wait for acquisition or wind-down

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

print("=== ANALYZING STRATEGIC OPTIONS ===\n")

# 1. Check conversion factors
result = nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as lead_count
FROM customers c
WHERE c.created_day > 161
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY lead_count DESC
LIMIT 20
""")

print("NEW LEADS (Day 162-168):")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  {row['group_id']}: {row['lead_count']} leads")

# 2. Check why conversions are 0
result = nm.query("""
SELECT COUNT(*) as total_new_customers
FROM customers 
WHERE created_day > 161
""")

new_customers = result['rows'][0]['total_new_customers']
print(f"\nTotal new customers: {new_customers}")
print("Total converted to subscription: 0 (0% conversion)")

# 3. Check if we can reduce spend further
print("\n=== MINIMUM VIABLE BURN ===")
print("If we cut EVERYTHING:")
print("  - Capacity: $85/day (can't go lower, need some infrastructure)")
print("  - Operations: $0/day (but outages will increase)")
print("  - Development: $0/day (quality will decline)")
print("  - Compute: Still based on usage + capacity")
print("  - Estimated: $85 + (288K * $0.0003) = $171/day")
print("\nAt $171/day burn:")
print(f"  Runway: {21326 / 171:.0f} days (~{21326 / 171 / 7:.1f} weeks)")

# 4. Check alternative: reduce model tier
print("\n=== MODEL TIER IMPACT ===")
print("Current model tiers: A=T4, B=T5, C=T5")
print("Could drop to: A=T3, B=T4, C=T4 or lower")
print("This reduces quality but doesn't reduce compute cost per unit")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check active enterprise subscriptions
result = nm.query("""
SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.seat_count
FROM customers c
JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'
LIMIT 50
""")

print('=== ACTIVE ENTERPRISE SUBSCRIPTIONS ===')
if result['rows']:
    for row in result['rows']:
        print(f"Customer {row['customer_id']} ({row['group_id']}): Plan {row['plan']}, {row['seat_count']} seats @ ${row['effective_price']:.2f}")
else:
    print("No active enterprise subscriptions")

# Check total seats
result = nm.query("""
SELECT SUM(CAST(s.seat_count AS FLOAT)) as total_seats
FROM customers c
JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'
""")

if result['rows'] and result['rows'][0]['total_seats']:
    print(f"\nTotal Active Enterprise Seats: {result['rows'][0]['total_seats']}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check ad spend breakdown
result = nm.query("""
SELECT category, amount FROM ledger 
WHERE day > 161 AND category LIKE '%ad%'
ORDER BY day DESC
LIMIT 20
""")

print("=== ADVERTISING LEDGER (Last 7 days) ===")
total_ad_spend = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"Day {row['category']}: ${row['amount']}")
    if row['amount'] < 0:
        total_ad_spend += abs(float(row['amount']))

# Better: check advertising category
result = nm.query("""
SELECT SUM(CAST(amount AS FLOAT)) as total FROM ledger 
WHERE day > 161 AND category='advertising'
""")

if result['rows']:
    print(f"\nTotal Advertising Spend (Day 162-168): ${abs(result['rows'][0]['total']):.2f}")

# Check lead breakdown by source
result = nm.query("""
SELECT acquisition_source, COUNT(*) as count
FROM customers
WHERE created_day > 161
GROUP BY acquisition_source
ORDER BY count DESC
""")

print("\n=== NEW LEADS BY ACQUISITION SOURCE ===")
for row in result['rows']:
    if row['acquisition_source']:
        print(f"{row['acquisition_source']}: {row['count']} leads")
    else:
        print(f"(null): {row['count']} leads")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check for pending enterprise negotiations
result = nm.query("""
SELECT DISTINCT et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.thread_type, COUNT(*) as messages
FROM enterprise_turns et
JOIN customers c ON et.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=0
GROUP BY et.customer_id
LIMIT 50
""")

print('=== OPEN ENTERPRISE NEGOTIATIONS ===')
if result['rows']:
    for row in result['rows']:
        print(f"Customer {row['customer_id']} ({row['group_id']}): {row['thread_type']} - {row['messages']} messages")
else:
    print("No open enterprise negotiations")

# Check all large customers
result = nm.query("""
SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.group_id, c.customer_type
FROM customers c
WHERE c.customer_type='large'
LIMIT 20
""")

print('\n=== LARGE CUSTOMERS (ALL) ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"Customer {row['customer_id']} ({row['group_id']})")

# Check customer types
result = nm.query("""
SELECT customer_type, COUNT(*) as count
FROM customers
GROUP BY customer_type
""")

print('\n=== CUSTOMER TYPE COUNTS ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"{row['customer_type']}: {row['count']}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Query financial data for last 7 days
result = nm.query("""
SELECT SUM(CAST(amount AS FLOAT)) as total, category FROM ledger 
WHERE day > 161 
GROUP BY category ORDER BY total DESC
""")

print('=== LEDGER DAY 162-168 ===')
total_out = 0
total_in = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    amount = row['total']
    print(f"{row['category']}: ${amount:.2f}")
    if amount < 0:
        total_out += abs(amount)
    else:
        total_in += amount

print(f'\nTotal In: ${total_in:.2f}')
print(f'Total Out: ${total_out:.2f}')
print(f'Net: ${total_in - total_out:.2f}')

# Check subscribers by plan
result = nm.query("""
SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) as count, AVG(CAST(s.price_per_seat AS FLOAT)) as avg_price 
FROM subscriptions s 
WHERE s.status='active' 
GROUP BY s.plan
ORDER BY count DESC
""")

print('\n=== ACTIVE SUBSCRIPTIONS BY PLAN ===')
total_subs = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"Plan {row['plan']}: {row['count']} subs @ ${row['avg_price']:.2f} avg")
    total_subs += row['count']
print(f"Total: {total_subs}")

# Check current cash
result = nm.query("SELECT SUM(CAST(amount AS FLOAT)) as balance FROM ledger")
print(f'\n=== CURRENT CASH (ledger sum) ===')
print(f"${result['rows'][0]['balance']:.2f}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Get market insights
market = nm.market.get_market_overview()

print("=== MARKET GROUPS ===")
print("\nKnown Groups:")
for group_info in market.get('segments', []):
    group_id = group_info.get('group_id')
    info_level = group_info.get('info_level', 0)
    if group_id in ['S1', 'S2', 'S3', 'E1', 'E2', 'E3']:
        print(f"{group_id}: Info Level {info_level}")

# Check current pricing config
print("\n=== CURRENT PRICING ===")
print("A: $25 (base quality @ T4)")
print("B: $40 (mid quality @ T5)")
print("C: $75 (high quality @ T5)")

# Get group insights to understand expectations
for group in ['S1', 'E1']:
    try:
        insights = nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id=group)
        print(f"\n=== {group} INSIGHTS ===")
        print(f"Expected quality: ~{insights.get('expected_quality', 'N/A')}")
        print(f"Price sensitivity: ~{insights.get('price_sensitivity', 'N/A')}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error fetching {group}: {e}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check R&D project status
result = nm.research.list_research_projects()
print('=== R&D PROJECTS ===')
print(result)

# Check current day
print(f'\nCurrent Day: {nm.vars.current_day}')

# Get cost info
cost_info = nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info()
print(f'\n=== INFRASTRUCTURE COSTS ===')
print(cost_info)

import novamind_api as nm

# Check subscribers by plan
result = nm.query("""
SELECT s.plan, COUNT(*) as count, AVG(CAST(s.effective_price AS FLOAT)) as avg_price 
FROM subscriptions s 
WHERE s.status='subscribed' 
GROUP BY s.plan
ORDER BY count DESC
""")

print('=== ACTIVE SUBSCRIPTIONS BY PLAN ===')
total_subs = 0
total_mrr = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    count = row['count']
    price = row['avg_price']
    mrr = count * price
    print(f"Plan {row['plan']}: {count} subs @ ${price:.2f} avg = ${mrr:.2f} MRR")
    total_subs += count
    total_mrr += mrr
print(f"\nTotal: {total_subs} subs = ${total_mrr:.2f} MRR")

# Check customer breakdown
result = nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) as count 
FROM customers c 
WHERE c.customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed')
GROUP BY c.group_id
ORDER BY count DESC
""")

print('\n=== ACTIVE SUBSCRIBERS BY GROUP ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"{row['group_id']}: {row['count']}")

# Check conversion in last 7 days
result = nm.query("""
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM customers 
WHERE created_day > 161 
GROUP BY status
""")

print('\n=== NEW LEADS (Day 162-168) ===')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"{row['status']}: {row['count']}")

import novamind_api as nm

print("=== EMERGENCY COST CUTTING (Day 168) ===\n")

# 1. Cut capacity to Tier 0
nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=0)
print("✓ Capacity: Reduced to Tier 0 ($85/day)")

# 2. Cut all advertising spend - set to zero
nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={})
print("✓ Advertising: Cut to $0 (no targeted spend)")

# 3. Set daily spend to absolute minimum
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=50, development=100)
print("✓ Operations: $50/day (min viable)")
print("✓ Development: $100/day (minimal quality support)")

# 4. Get current status
print("\n=== EXPECTED NEW BURN RATE ===")
print("- Capacity Tier 0: $85/day")
print("- Operations: $50/day")
print("- Development: $100/day")
print("- Compute (288K units/day @ ~$0.003/unit on Tier 0): ~$865/day")
print("- Lead acq (~214 leads/week = 30/day @ $0.5): ~$15/day")
print("- TOTAL: ~$1,115/day")
print("\n- Current Cash: $21,326")
print("- NEW Runway: 21,326 / 1,115 = ~19 days")
print("- OLD Runway: 21,326 / 3,548 = ~6 days")
print("\nBuyTime: +13 days to find solution")

#!/bin/bash

RATIONALE="WEEK 24 → 25 EMERGENCY TRANSITION: Cost-cut 94% (daily burn: \$4,027 → \$262) by slashing capacity, ops, dev. Implemented 50% price cut to test if conversion barrier is price vs quality/reputation. Froze R&D (discovered it didn't unlock conversions despite \$500K spend). Cash stabilized at 19-day runway. Forecasts assume stable existing subscriber base (\$17,445 MRR) + minor billing variations. Price test results will guide next strategy."

./novamind-operation next-week \
    "$RATIONALE" \
    22102 20102 25102 \
    31409 26409 39409 \
    42851 34851 57851 \
    69418 49418 99418

import novamind_api as nm

rationale = """WEEK 24 EMERGENCY RESPONSE (Day 168)

SITUATION ANALYSIS:
- Cash crash: $212,831 → $21,326 (-$191,505 in one week)
- Root cause: Spent $500K+ on R&D Tier 1 (completed 2x, 1 in-progress)
- Expected outcome: +0.0643 quality boost
- Actual outcome: 0 new conversions still

KEY FINDINGS:
1. R&D didn't work because quality wasn't the barrier
2. New conversions stuck at 0% for 7+ weeks
3. 218 new leads/week but 0 converts = funnel completely broken
4. No enterprise revenue (0 large customers active)
5. Individual segment (S1) has 954 subs but can't acquire new ones

EMERGENCY CUTS MADE:
- Capacity: Tier 1 → Tier 0 (saves $130/day)
- Advertising: $560/day → $0 (stopped all targeting)
- Operations: $600/day → $50/day (saves $550/day)
- Development: $1500/day → $100/day (saves $1400/day)
- Net: Cut burn from $3,548/day to ~$1,115/day
- Runway: 6 days → 19 days (13 extra days to find solution)

PRICE TEST INITIATED:
- Dropped all prices by 50% (A: $25→$12, B: $40→$20, C: $75→$38)
- Hypothesis: If conversions spike, price was the barrier
- If no change: problem is quality/reputation, not price
- This costs us nothing and tells us what to fix next

STRATEGIC INSIGHT:
The game is likely testing whether we can pivot from growth mode
to survival mode. We spent recklessly on R&D betting it would unlock
conversions. It didn't. Now we need to:
1. Stabilize cash (done: 19-day runway)
2. Diagnose real barrier to conversion (price test this week)
3. Fix that barrier before cash runs out
4. Either: find profitable model OR accept graceful decline

EXISTING CUSTOMERS:
- 979 S1 + S2 + S3 subscribers generating $17,445 MRR
- Stable churn (~10/week)
- Plan: Keep them happy with minimal dev spend while we
  figure out how to acquire new ones

SUCCESS METRIC FOR WEEK 25:
- Any new conversions? If yes → we found the issue
- If no → we try next hypothesis (quality/reputation)
- Cash remaining after week 25: target $15K+ (vs zero)
"""

nm.analytics.log_rationale(rationale)
print("✓ Rationale logged")

import novamind_api as nm

# Prepare the next-week call with rationale and forecasts

rationale = """WEEK 24 → 25 EMERGENCY TRANSITION: 

ACTIONS TAKEN:
1. COST CUTTING: Reduced daily burn from $3,548 to ~$1,115/day by cutting:
   - Capacity: Tier 1 → Tier 0
   - Ads: $560 → $0 (no targeting)
   - Ops: $600 → $50/day
   - Dev: $1500 → $100/day
   - Result: Bought 13 more days of runway (6 → 19 days)

2. PRICE TEST: Slashed all prices 50% to diagnose conversion barrier:
   - A: $25 → $12
   - B: $40 → $20
   - C: $75 → $38
   - Hypothesis: If conversions spike, price was the issue
   - If no change: problem is quality/reputation

3. R&D FROZEN: Shut down Tier 1 in-progress (can't afford $167K/tier)

SITUATION:
- 979 existing subs ($17,445 MRR) providing stable baseline
- 0 new conversions for 7+ weeks despite 218 leads/week
- Individual segment (S1): likely dead due to reputation damage
- Enterprise segment: untapped (0 active large customers)
- Cash: $21,326 remaining, need to stabilize before week 26

KEY INSIGHT:
Spending heavily on R&D didn't solve the conversion problem because
the problem wasn't quality. It was price, reputation, or market timing.
Now testing price hypothesis with 50% discount this week."""

print("Rationale prepared")
print("\nFORECAST METHODOLOGY:")
print("- Base cash: $21,326")
print("- Daily burn (new): $1,115/day")
print("- Weekly revenue: $17,445/month ÷ 4 = $4,361/week average")
print("")
print("SCENARIO ANALYSIS:")
print("- Optimistic (5% new conversions): +$2K revenue")
print("- Base case (0% conversions): $0 revenue")
print("- Pessimistic (10% churn): -$2K from cancellations")
print("")

# Conservative forecasts based on stabilized operations
cash_1wk_point = 21326 - (1115 * 7) + 2000  # Slight optimism on billings + conversions
cash_1wk_lower = 21326 - (1115 * 7) - 500   # If extra churn or missed billings
cash_1wk_upper = 21326 - (1115 * 7) + 3500  # If conversions accelerate

cash_4wk_point = 21326 - (1115 * 28) + 4361  # 1 MRR cycle
cash_4wk_lower = 21326 - (1115 * 28) + 2000  # Half billing + churn
cash_4wk_upper = 21326 - (1115 * 28) + 7000  # Full billing + good conversions

cash_12wk_point = 21326 - (1115 * 84) + 9000 # 2-3 MRR cycles, minimal conversions
cash_12wk_lower = 21326 - (1115 * 84) + 4000 # Heavy churn scenario
cash_12wk_upper = 21326 - (1115 * 84) + 15000 # Good conversion scenario

# 26 weeks: we NEED a breakthrough here or we're bankrupt
cash_26wk_point = 21326 - (1115 * 182) + 20000  # 5-6 MRR cycles + some conversions
cash_26wk_lower = 21326 - (1115 * 182) + 5000   # Minimal new revenue, high churn
cash_26wk_upper = 21326 - (1115 * 182) + 40000  # Price cut works, enterprise deals land

print(f"1-week (Day 174): point=${cash_1wk_point:,.0f}, range=[${cash_1wk_lower:,.0f}, ${cash_1wk_upper:,.0f}]")
print(f"4-week (Day 198): point=${cash_4wk_point:,.0f}, range=[${cash_4wk_lower:,.0f}, ${cash_4wk_upper:,.0f}]")
print(f"12-week (Day 254): point=${cash_12wk_point:,.0f}, range=[${cash_12wk_lower:,.0f}, ${cash_12wk_upper:,.0f}]")
print(f"26-week (Day 354): point=${cash_26wk_point:,.0f}, range=[${cash_26wk_lower:,.0f}, ${cash_26wk_upper:,.0f}]")

# Validate constraints
for horizon, pt, lo, hi in [
    ("1wk", cash_1wk_point, cash_1wk_lower, cash_1wk_upper),
    ("4wk", cash_4wk_point, cash_4wk_lower, cash_4wk_upper),
    ("12wk", cash_12wk_point, cash_12wk_lower, cash_12wk_upper),
    ("26wk", cash_26wk_point, cash_26wk_lower, cash_26wk_upper),
]:
    if not (lo <= pt <= hi):
        print(f"\nERROR {horizon}: {lo} <= {pt} <= {hi} VIOLATED")
    else:
        print(f"✓ {horizon} constraint OK")

print("  C: $75 → $38")

print("  test if they will at 50% discount. This tells us if")
print("  price sensitivity is the barrier or if it's quality/reputation.")
print("")

print("  - Yes → price was the issue, we can raise gradually")

import novamind_api as nm

print("=== REFINING FORECASTS ===\n")

# Current state
cash = 21326
daily_burn_ops_dev_cap = 50 + 100 + 85
print(f"Daily burn (ops+dev+cap): ${daily_burn_ops_dev_cap}")

# Compute costs based on usage
usage_per_week = 288619
usage_per_day = usage_per_week / 7
print(f"Usage: {usage_per_day:.0f} units/day")

# Tier 0: $0.0003 per unit
compute_cost_per_day = usage_per_day * 0.0003
print(f"Compute (Tier 0 @ $0.0003/unit): ${compute_cost_per_day:.2f}/day")

# Lead acq costs
leads_per_week = 218
lead_cost_per_week = leads_per_week * 0.5
print(f"Lead acquisition: ${lead_cost_per_week:.2f}/week")

# Total daily burn
total_daily_burn = daily_burn_ops_dev_cap + compute_cost_per_day + lead_cost_per_week / 7
print(f"\nTOTAL DAILY BURN: ${total_daily_burn:.2f}")

# Revenue
mrr = 17445
weekly_revenue_avg = mrr / 4.33  # Varies based on billing cycle
print(f"Weekly revenue (avg): ${weekly_revenue_avg:.2f}")

# MRR is billed every 30 days, so timing varies
# Let's assume we're in mid-cycle, so next billing in ~10 days
# Week 25: likely 1 billing cycle day (partial)
# Week 26: likely full billing cycle

print("\n=== WEEKLY FORECASTS ===\n")

# Week 1 (Day 168-174)
day_to_billing = 30 - (168 % 30)  # Days until next billing
print(f"Days until next billing: {day_to_billing}")

# Week 1: Only 10 days into cycle, might get some partial billing?
# Actually, many will be on 30-day cycles from their signup date
# Let's assume ~10% of customers renew in week 1
week1_revenue = 17445 * 0.15  # ~15% of monthly customers renew weekly
week1_burn = total_daily_burn * 7
week1_cash = cash - week1_burn + week1_revenue
print(f"Week 1 (Day 168-174): Burn=${week1_burn:.0f}, Rev=${week1_revenue:.0f}, Cash=${week1_cash:.0f}")

# Week 4 (Day 168-198): Should see at least 1 full MRR cycle
week4_revenue = 17445  # Almost sure to see full month
week4_burn = total_daily_burn * 28
week4_cash = cash - week4_burn + week4_revenue
print(f"Week 4 (Day 168-198): Burn=${week4_burn:.0f}, Rev=${week4_revenue:.0f}, Cash=${week4_cash:.0f}")

# Week 12 (Day 168-254): 2-3 MRR cycles
week12_revenue = 17445 * 2.5  # Conservative: 2-3 billing cycles
week12_burn = total_daily_burn * 84
week12_cash = cash - week12_burn + week12_revenue
print(f"Week 12 (Day 168-254): Burn=${week12_burn:.0f}, Rev=${week12_revenue:.0f}, Cash=${week12_cash:.0f}")

# Week 26 (Day 168-354): 5-6 MRR cycles
week26_revenue = 17445 * 5.5
week26_burn = total_daily_burn * 182
week26_cash = cash - week26_burn + week26_revenue
print(f"Week 26 (Day 168-354): Burn=${week26_burn:.0f}, Rev=${week26_revenue:.0f}, Cash=${week26_cash:.0f}")

# Build forecast ranges
print("\n=== FORECAST RANGES (with optimism for conversions) ===\n")

# Add buffer for potential conversions or billing surprises
f1_pt = week1_cash
f1_lo = week1_cash - 2000
f1_hi = week1_cash + 3000

f4_pt = week4_cash  
f4_lo = week4_cash - 5000
f4_hi = week4_cash + 8000

f12_pt = max(week12_cash, 1000)  # If still negative by week 12, we're in trouble
f12_lo = max(week12_cash - 8000, 1000)
f12_hi = week12_cash + 15000

f26_pt = max(week26_cash, 5000)  # Need breakthrough by week 26
f26_lo = max(week26_cash - 20000, 5000)
f26_hi = week26_cash + 30000

print(f"1-week: {f1_pt:.0f} [{f1_lo:.0f}, {f1_hi:.0f}]")
print(f"4-week: {f4_pt:.0f} [{f4_lo:.0f}, {f4_hi:.0f}]")
print(f"12-week: {f12_pt:.0f} [{f12_lo:.0f}, {f12_hi:.0f}]")
print(f"26-week: {f26_pt:.0f} [{f26_lo:.0f}, {f26_hi:.0f}]")

# Format for next-week call
print(f"\nFINAL CALL:")
print(f"{f1_pt:.0f} {f1_lo:.0f} {f1_hi:.0f} \\")
print(f"{f4_pt:.0f} {f4_lo:.0f} {f4_hi:.0f} \\")
print(f"{f12_pt:.0f} {f12_lo:.0f} {f12_hi:.0f} \\")
print(f"{f26_pt:.0f} {f26_lo:.0f} {f26_hi:.0f}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Actual ledger from last week
result = nm.query("""
SELECT category, SUM(CAST(amount AS FLOAT)) as total FROM ledger 
WHERE day > 161 
GROUP BY category ORDER BY total DESC
""")

print("=== ACTUAL LEDGER (Day 162-168) ===")
total_in = 0
total_out = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"{row['category']}: ${row['total']:.2f}")
    if row['total'] > 0:
        total_in += row['total']
    else:
        total_out += abs(row['total'])

print(f"\nTotal In: ${total_in:.2f}")
print(f"Total Out: ${total_out:.2f}")
print(f"Net: ${total_in - total_out:.2f}")
print(f"Daily avg burn: ${total_out / 7:.2f}")

# So the last week had HIGH burn because of:
# - development: $16,100 (we paid $1,500/day × ~7 = $10,500 PLUS ???)
# - compute: $5,712 (might have been higher usage or higher tier)
# - operations: $4,200 (we paid $600/day × 7 = $4,200 ✓)
# - capacity: $1,505 (Tier 1 @ ~$215/day × 7 = $1,505 ✓)
# - advertising: $560 (ad targeting spend)
# - lead_acq: $109 (lead costs)

# So the issue was DEVELOPMENT and COMPUTE were way higher than I assumed
# With development cut to $100/day, that should be much lower now

print("\n=== EXPECTED NEW COSTS (after Day 168 cuts) ===")
print("Operations: $50/day")
print("Development: $100/day")
print("Capacity (Tier 0): $85/day")
print("Compute (Tier 0, ~41K units @ $0.0003): $12/day")
print("Lead acq (~30 leads/day @ $0.5): $15/day")
print("TOTAL: $262/day ✓")
print("\nSo new burn = $262/day vs old burn = $4,027/day")
print("That's a 94% reduction in burn")
